panel.title: Plecost v4.0 Scan Report
panel.blocked: BLOCKED — target returned HTTP 403, scan aborted

descriptions.rest_api: Check REST API exposure and misconfigs
descriptions.debug_exposure: Detect debug mode and PHP exposure
descriptions.content_analysis: Detect card skimming and hardcoded secrets
descriptions.cves: Correlate found software with CVE database
descriptions.wp_ecommerce: Detect WP eCommerce and check for vulnerabilities
descriptions.magecart: Detect Magecart/card-skimming scripts on checkout pages
descriptions.webshells: Detect webshells and backdoors in wp-content

pc_fp_001.title: WordPress version disclosed via meta generator tag
pc_fp_001.description: The WordPress version is exposed in the HTML meta generator tag, allowing attackers to target known vulnerabilities for that version.
pc_fp_001.remediation: Remove the generator meta tag. Add to functions.php: remove_action('wp_head', 'wp_generator');

pc_fp_002.title: WordPress version disclosed via readme.html
pc_fp_002.description: The /readme.html file is publicly accessible and discloses the WordPress version.

pc_usr_001.title: User enumeration via REST API
pc_usr_001.description: The WordPress REST API endpoint /wp-json/wp/v2/users exposes a list of usernames without authentication.
pc_usr_001.remediation: Restrict the REST API users endpoint. Add to functions.php: add_filter('rest_endpoints', function($e){ unset($e['/wp/v2/users']); return $e; });

pc_usr_002.title: User enumeration via author archives
pc_usr_002.description: WordPress author archive URLs (/?author=N) redirect to /author/username/, exposing valid usernames.
pc_usr_002.remediation: Redirect /?author=N to homepage. Add rewrite rules or use a security plugin.

pc_xmlrpc_001.title: XML-RPC endpoint is accessible
pc_xmlrpc_001.description: The xmlrpc.php endpoint is publicly accessible. It can be abused for brute force attacks and DoS amplification.
pc_xmlrpc_001.remediation:
Disable XML-RPC if not needed. Add to .htaccess: <Files xmlrpc.php>
Order Deny,Allow
Deny from all
</Files>

pc_xmlrpc_002.title: XML-RPC pingback.ping enabled (DoS amplification)
pc_xmlrpc_002.description: The pingback.ping method is enabled in XML-RPC, allowing attackers to use your site as a DoS amplification vector against third parties.
pc_xmlrpc_002.remediation: Disable pingbacks: add_filter('xmlrpc_methods', function($m){ unset($m['pingback.ping']); return $m; });

pc_xmlrpc_003.title: XML-RPC system.listMethods exposed
pc_xmlrpc_003.description: system.listMethods is available, allowing attackers to enumerate all available XML-RPC methods.
pc_xmlrpc_003.remediation: Disable XML-RPC entirely if not needed.

pc_xmlrpc_004.title: XML-RPC system.multicall enabled (Brute-force amplification)
pc_xmlrpc_004.description: The system.multicall method is enabled in XML-RPC, allowing attackers to bypass rate limits and perform massive brute-force attacks by sending thousands of credentials in a single request.
pc_xmlrpc_004.remediation: Disable system.multicall or restrict XML-RPC access entirely.

pc_xmlrpc_005.title: XML-RPC Brute-Force Protection Missing
pc_xmlrpc_005.description: The xmlrpc.php endpoint is accessible and does not appear to enforce rate limiting on failed authentication attempts. It successfully processed consecutive failed logins.
pc_xmlrpc_005.remediation: Implement rate limiting via a WAF, security plugin, or Fail2Ban, or disable XML-RPC entirely.

pc_xmlrpc_info.title: XML-RPC Guardrails Active
pc_xmlrpc_info.description: The xmlrpc.php endpoint is accessible, but active defenses (WAF/Rate Limiting) successfully blocked repeated authentication attempts.
pc_xmlrpc_info.remediation: No immediate action required. Defenses are working as expected.

pc_rest_001.title: REST API link exposed in HTML head
pc_rest_001.description: The WordPress REST API discovery link is present in the HTML head, confirming WordPress installation.

pc_rest_002.title: REST API oEmbed exposes user information
pc_rest_002.description: The oEmbed endpoint leaks author information.
pc_rest_002.remediation: Disable oEmbed or restrict the endpoint.

pc_rest_003.title: REST API CORS misconfiguration
pc_rest_003.description: The REST API returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * allowing any origin to read API responses.
pc_rest_003.remediation: Restrict CORS headers in your web server configuration.

pc_mcfg_001.title: wp-config.php is publicly accessible
pc_mcfg_001.description: wp-config.php is accessible and exposes database credentials and security keys.
pc_mcfg_001.remediation: Move wp-config.php one directory above webroot or restrict with .htaccess.

pc_mcfg_002.title: wp-config.php backup exposed
pc_mcfg_002.description: A backup of wp-config.php is publicly accessible.
pc_mcfg_002.remediation: Delete all backup files of wp-config.php from the server.

pc_mcfg_003.title: .env file exposed
pc_mcfg_003.description: .env file is accessible and may expose API keys and secrets.
pc_mcfg_003.remediation: Restrict .env access via web server configuration.

pc_mcfg_004.title: .git directory exposed
pc_mcfg_004.description: The .git directory is accessible, potentially exposing source code and credentials.
pc_mcfg_004.remediation: Deny access to .git in your web server config.

pc_mcfg_005.title: debug.log exposed
pc_mcfg_005.description: WordPress debug log is publicly accessible and may contain sensitive paths and data.
pc_mcfg_005.remediation: Delete debug.log and set WP_DEBUG_LOG to false.

pc_mcfg_006.title: SQL backup file exposed
pc_mcfg_006.description: A database backup file is publicly accessible.
pc_mcfg_006.remediation: Remove backup files from the webroot immediately.

pc_mcfg_007.title: wp-admin/install.php accessible
pc_mcfg_007.remediation: Restrict access to install.php after installation.

pc_mcfg_008.title: wp-admin/upgrade.php accessible
pc_mcfg_008.remediation: Restrict access to upgrade.php after updates.

pc_mcfg_009.title: readme.html discloses WP version
pc_mcfg_009.description: readme.html is accessible and may disclose WordPress version.

pc_mcfg_012.title: wp-cron.php externally accessible
pc_mcfg_012.description: wp-cron.php can be triggered by anyone.
pc_mcfg_012.remediation: define('DISABLE_WP_CRON', true); and use real server cron.

pc_hdr_002.title: Missing X-Frame-Options
pc_hdr_002.description: X-Frame-Options header is absent, allowing clickjacking.

pc_hdr_006.title: Missing Permissions-Policy
pc_hdr_006.remediation: Add a Permissions-Policy header restricting unused browser features.

pc_hdr_007.title: Server header discloses version
pc_hdr_007.description: The Server header reveals web server software and version.
pc_hdr_007.remediation: Configure web server to suppress version info.

pc_hdr_008.title: X-Powered-By discloses PHP version
pc_hdr_008.description: PHP version is exposed via X-Powered-By header.

pc_ssl_001.title: HTTP to HTTPS redirect missing
pc_ssl_001.description: The site does not redirect HTTP to HTTPS.
pc_ssl_001.remediation: Configure 301 redirect from HTTP to HTTPS in web server config.

pc_ssl_002.title: Invalid or expired SSL certificate
pc_ssl_002.description: The SSL certificate is invalid or expired.

pc_ssl_003.title: HSTS not configured
pc_ssl_003.remediation: Enable HSTS in web server configuration.

pc_dbg_001.title: WP_DEBUG active — PHP errors exposed
pc_dbg_001.description: PHP error messages are visible in responses, indicating WP_DEBUG=true.
pc_dbg_001.remediation: Set define('WP_DEBUG', false); in wp-config.php for production.

pc_dbg_003.title: PHP version exposed via X-Powered-By
pc_dbg_003.description: PHP version is disclosed in the X-Powered-By response header.

pc_cnt_001.title: Potential card skimming script
pc_cnt_001.description: A script with card skimming patterns was detected.
pc_cnt_001.remediation: Investigate and remove the suspicious script immediately. Check for site compromise.

pc_cnt_002.title: Suspicious external iframe
pc_cnt_002.description: An external iframe from an unexpected domain was found.
pc_cnt_002.remediation: Review all external iframes. Remove unauthorized ones.

pc_cnt_003.title: Hardcoded API key in page source
pc_cnt_003.description: An API key pattern was found in public page source.
pc_cnt_003.remediation: Move secrets to server-side config. Never expose keys in client-side code.

pc_waf_001.title: WAF/CDN detected
pc_waf_001.description: A WAF or CDN was detected. Some findings may be incomplete.
pc_waf_001.remediation: No action needed. WAF is a positive security control.

pc_auth_001.title: Successful authentication
pc_auth_001.remediation: Change default credentials. Use a strong unique password.

pc_wc_000.title: WooCommerce installation summary
pc_wc_000.description: A summary of the WooCommerce installation was collected.
pc_wc_000.remediation: Review WooCommerce configuration and harden as needed.

pc_wc_001.title: WooCommerce detected via Store API
pc_wc_001.description: WooCommerce was detected on this site via the Store API endpoint.
pc_wc_001.remediation: No action required unless WooCommerce is not intentionally installed.

pc_wc_002.title: WooCommerce version disclosed via readme.txt
pc_wc_002.description: The WooCommerce plugin readme.txt file is publicly accessible and discloses the WooCommerce version.

pc_wc_003.title: WooCommerce REST API namespaces exposed
pc_wc_003.description: The WooCommerce REST API namespaces are publicly listed, confirming WooCommerce installation and revealing available API versions.
pc_wc_003.remediation: Restrict the REST API namespace discovery endpoint or require authentication.

pc_wc_004.title: WooCommerce REST API: customer list exposed without authentication
pc_wc_004.description: The WooCommerce REST API /wc/v3/customers endpoint returns customer data without requiring authentication, exposing PII.
pc_wc_004.remediation: Require authentication for the WooCommerce REST API. Review WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API.

pc_wc_005.title: WooCommerce REST API: order list exposed without authentication
pc_wc_005.description: The WooCommerce REST API /wc/v3/orders endpoint returns order data without requiring authentication.
pc_wc_005.remediation: Require authentication for the WooCommerce REST API.

pc_wc_006.title: WooCommerce REST API: coupon list exposed without authentication
pc_wc_006.description: The WooCommerce REST API /wc/v3/coupons endpoint returns coupon codes without requiring authentication.
pc_wc_006.remediation: Require authentication for the WooCommerce REST API.

pc_wc_007.title: WooCommerce REST API: system status exposed without authentication
pc_wc_007.description: The WooCommerce REST API /wc/v3/system_status endpoint returns detailed system information without authentication.
pc_wc_007.remediation: Require authentication for the WooCommerce REST API.

pc_wc_008.title: WooCommerce logs directory listing enabled
pc_wc_008.description: Directory listing is enabled in /wp-content/uploads/wc-logs/, exposing WooCommerce log files.
pc_wc_008.remediation: Disable directory listing and restrict access to the wc-logs directory.

pc_wc_009.title: WooCommerce uploads directory accessible
pc_wc_009.description: The WooCommerce uploads directory is publicly accessible and may expose sensitive files.
pc_wc_009.remediation: Add an .htaccess or nginx rule to restrict direct file access in the uploads directory.

pc_wc_010.title: WooCommerce Payments plugin detected
pc_wc_010.description: The WooCommerce Payments plugin was detected on this site.
pc_wc_010.remediation: Keep WooCommerce Payments updated to the latest version.

pc_wc_011.title: WooCommerce Stripe Gateway plugin detected
pc_wc_011.description: The WooCommerce Stripe Gateway plugin was detected on this site.
pc_wc_011.remediation: Keep the Stripe Gateway plugin updated to the latest version.

pc_wc_012.title: WooCommerce system status retrieved (authenticated)
pc_wc_012.description: WooCommerce system status was successfully retrieved using authenticated API credentials.
pc_wc_012.remediation: No action required. Review system status for any misconfigurations.

pc_wc_013.title: WooCommerce payment gateway configuration exposed (authenticated)
pc_wc_013.description: WooCommerce payment gateway configuration was retrieved via authenticated API, potentially exposing API keys and secrets.
pc_wc_013.remediation: Restrict API key permissions and rotate any exposed credentials.

pc_wc_020.title: CVE-2023-28121: WooCommerce Payments authentication bypass
pc_wc_020.description: CVE-2023-28121 (CVSS 9.8): A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in WooCommerce Payments allows unauthenticated attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators.
pc_wc_020.remediation: Update WooCommerce Payments to version 5.6.2 or later immediately.

pc_wc_021.title: CVE-2023-34000: WooCommerce Stripe Gateway IDOR (PII disclosure)
pc_wc_021.description: CVE-2023-34000 (CVSS 7.5): An Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability in the WooCommerce Stripe Gateway plugin allows unauthenticated attackers to access other users' PII.
pc_wc_021.remediation: Update the WooCommerce Stripe Gateway plugin to version 7.4.1 or later.

pc_wpec_000.title: WP eCommerce: installation summary
pc_wpec_000.description: Summary of the WP eCommerce plugin analysis.
pc_wpec_000.remediation: See individual findings for remediation details.

pc_wpec_001.title: WP eCommerce detected
pc_wpec_001.description: The WP eCommerce (wp-e-commerce) plugin is installed and active.
pc_wpec_001.remediation: WP eCommerce is abandoned. Consider migrating to WooCommerce.

pc_wpec_002.title: WP eCommerce: version exposed via readme.txt
pc_wpec_002.description: The WP eCommerce version is publicly accessible via readme.txt.
pc_wpec_002.remediation: Block access to readme.txt via web server rules.

pc_wpec_003.title: WP eCommerce: abandoned plugin with unpatched CVEs
pc_wpec_003.description: WP eCommerce has not received updates since 2020 (last version: 3.15.1). All current installations are vulnerable to CVE-2024-1514 (SQL Injection via ChronoPay callback) and CVE-2026-1235 (PHP Object Injection), with no patch available.
pc_wpec_003.remediation: Uninstall WP eCommerce immediately and migrate to an actively maintained alternative such as WooCommerce.

pc_wpec_004.title: WP eCommerce: ChronoPay payment gateway detected
pc_wpec_004.description: The ChronoPay payment gateway is installed in WP eCommerce.
pc_wpec_004.remediation: Verify that the ChronoPay gateway is securely configured.

pc_wpec_005.title: WP eCommerce: directory listing enabled in plugin directory
pc_wpec_005.description: Directory listing is enabled in the WP eCommerce plugin directory, exposing its file structure.
pc_wpec_005.remediation: Disable directory listing on the web server.

pc_wpec_006.title: WP eCommerce: uploads directory accessible
pc_wpec_006.description: The WP eCommerce uploads directory is publicly accessible.
pc_wpec_006.remediation: Add an .htaccess file denying direct access to the wpsc/ directory.

pc_wpec_007.title: WP eCommerce: digital downloads directory exposed
pc_wpec_007.description: The digital products directory is publicly accessible, exposing paid download files.
pc_wpec_007.remediation: Configure the web server to deny direct access to the wpsc/digital/ directory.

pc_wpec_008.title: WP eCommerce: database backup script accessible
pc_wpec_008.description: The db-backup.php script in the WP eCommerce admin panel is accessible without authentication.
pc_wpec_008.remediation: Block direct access to files in wpsc-admin/ via web server rules.

pc_wpec_009.title: WP eCommerce: log viewer accessible
pc_wpec_009.description: The display-log.php log viewer in WP eCommerce is accessible without authentication.
pc_wpec_009.remediation: Block direct access to files in wpsc-admin/ via web server rules.

pc_wpec_010.title: WP eCommerce: ChronoPay callback endpoint exposed
pc_wpec_010.description: The ChronoPay callback endpoint responds without signature verification.
pc_wpec_010.remediation: Verify ChronoPay configuration and apply signature validation on callbacks.

pc_wpec_020.title: CVE-2024-1514: WP eCommerce ChronoPay SQL Injection
pc_wpec_020.description: The ChronoPay callback endpoint is vulnerable to SQL Injection (CVE-2024-1514).
pc_wpec_020.remediation: Uninstall WP eCommerce. No patch is available.

pc_wpec_021.title: CVE-2026-1235: WP eCommerce PHP Object Injection
pc_wpec_021.description: The WP eCommerce AJAX endpoint is vulnerable to PHP Object Injection (CVE-2026-1235).
pc_wpec_021.remediation: Uninstall WP eCommerce. No patch is available.

pc_mgc_000.title: Magecart scan summary
pc_mgc_000.description: The Magecart detection module ran on the eCommerce checkout pages. This finding summarizes what was scanned.
pc_mgc_000.remediation: No action required. Review other PC-MGC-* findings for actionable issues.

pc_mgc_001.title: Known Magecart domain script on checkout page
pc_mgc_001.description: A script from a known Magecart card-skimming domain was found loading on a checkout page. This is a strong indicator of a supply-chain compromise targeting payment card data.
pc_mgc_001.remediation: Immediately remove the malicious script. Audit your theme and plugin files for injected code. Reset all WordPress credentials and rotate payment API keys. Notify your payment processor.

pc_mgc_002.title: Known dropper domain script on checkout page
pc_mgc_002.description: A script from a known dropper domain was found on a checkout page. Dropper scripts typically load secondary payloads, including card skimmers.
pc_mgc_002.remediation: Immediately remove the malicious script and audit all JavaScript on the checkout flow. Check for secondary payloads or additional injections.

pc_mgc_003.title: Known exfiltrator domain script on checkout page
pc_mgc_003.description: A script from a known data exfiltration domain was found on a checkout page. Exfiltrator scripts send captured data (including payment card details) to attacker-controlled servers.
pc_mgc_003.remediation: Remove the script immediately. Review all external script sources on the checkout page. Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) to restrict script origins.

pc_mgc_004.title: Known Magecart domain script on non-checkout page
pc_mgc_004.description: A script from a known Magecart-related domain was found on a non-checkout page. While lower risk than checkout exposure, this may indicate site compromise.
pc_mgc_004.remediation: Investigate the script source. Remove if unauthorized. Check if the domain appears on checkout pages as well.

pc_wsh_001.title: Known webshell path is accessible
pc_wsh_001.description: A file matching a known webshell filename was found accessible via HTTP.
pc_wsh_001.remediation: Remove the file immediately. Audit all wp-content directories. Rotate all credentials.

pc_wsh_100.title: PHP file executable in wp-content/uploads
pc_wsh_100.description: A PHP file is accessible and executable inside wp-content/uploads, which should never execute PHP.
pc_wsh_100.remediation: Remove the file. Add .htaccess to uploads/ to deny PHP execution.

pc_wsh_150.title: Suspicious PHP file in wp-content/mu-plugins
pc_wsh_150.description: A PHP file matching a known backdoor name was found in must-use plugins directory.
pc_wsh_150.remediation: Review and remove unexpected files from wp-content/mu-plugins/.

pc_wsh_200.title: Webshell family fingerprint matched in HTTP response
pc_wsh_200.description: The response from a PHP file matches a known webshell family signature.
pc_wsh_200.remediation: The site is compromised. Take offline, remove webshell, audit all files, rotate all credentials.

pc_wsh_250.title: WordPress core file has been modified
pc_wsh_250.description: A WordPress core file does not match the official checksum for the installed version.
pc_wsh_250.remediation: Verify if the modification is authorized. If not, restore the file from a clean WP installation.

pc_wsh_300.title: Unrecognized plugin detected via WordPress REST API
pc_wsh_300.description: The WordPress REST API reports an active plugin that was not detected during passive scanning.
pc_wsh_300.remediation: Verify the plugin is intentionally installed. If unknown, remove it and inspect for malicious code.

pc_pre_001.title: Target blocked — HTTP 403 on pre-flight probe
pc_pre_001.description: The target URL returned HTTP 403 on the initial probe. The scan was aborted.
pc_pre_001.remediation: Check if the target is accessible and you have authorization to scan it.
